rag_rules: Fix unresolved identifiers or invalid helper declarations causing 'named thing must be an expression with a type' errors by referencing only visible AADL features or locally declared eq helpers.
Avoid using implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts; place implementation-level bindings in implementation scopes.
generic_rules: When validator reports type errors, trace the first unresolved identifier on the same line and resolve it by correcting the reference or removing the invalid clause.
Ensure AGREE annex expressions reference only visible features or locally declared helpers to prevent type resolution failures.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE guarantees must reference valid AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex; do not use undefined identifiers.
Component type contracts should avoid implementation-specific paths; use visible ports and variables for type-level guarantees.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve type errors; instead, correct or remove invalid AGREE clauses.
Do not reference subcomponent ports in component type annexes unless represented through valid variables or outputs.
